purpose: Declare how customer-confirmed low-code configuration is promoted from runtime edits to the replayable smart_construction_custom baseline.

module_boundary.responsibility: customer-confirmed menu, form, field, role, initialization, and visible-surface preferences that must survive fresh install, upgrade, daily-dev sync, and production deployment
forbidden_responsibilities: industry-standard defaults shared by all customers

promotion_rule.customer_confirmed_destination: smart_construction_custom or an explicit customer module
promotion_rule.rule: A low-code save is not a delivery baseline until the confirmed result is captured in a replayable module asset, migration, or idempotent initializer.

acceptance_policy.fresh_install: post_init_hook must apply platform initialization, business policy, user data baseline, user menu preferences, user form preferences, and low-code source_status backfill.
acceptance_policy.upgrade: custom module migration must backfill explicit source_status for existing configuration contracts.
acceptance_policy.runtime: runtime low-code boundary guard must see protected configuration menus through extension/config sources and reject ordinary-user visibility of global low-code configuration entries.
acceptance_policy.cross_environment: business configuration snapshots are the handoff artifact for comparing local, daily-dev, prod-sim, and production customer configuration state.
